controls.label: Does your business have a Customer Due Diligence — Partnerships process?
controls.remediation: Your organisation does not have a Customer Due Diligence — Partnerships process in place. You must establish a documented Customer Due Diligence — Partnerships process to meet your AML/CTF obligations. Review the controls below and implement the required procedures before completing this assessment.

controls.label: Does your AML/CTF program include risk-based systems and controls to verify that the partnership exists and that all partners' names have been provided?
controls.detail-label: Describe your risk-based systems and controls
controls.remediation: Your Customer Identification Procedure for Partnerships does not include the required risk-based systems and controls to verify partnership existence and partner names. Implement controls that enable you to be reasonably satisfied that the partnership exists and that all partner names have been provided in accordance with rule 4.5.3(5), as required under rules 4.5.2(1) and 4.5.2(2).

controls.label: Does your procedure collect the minimum KYC information required for partnerships?

checklist-items.label: Full business name (if any) as registered under State or Territory business names legislation
Country in which the partnership was established
Information required to be collected about one of the partners (as per individual customer ID procedure)
Full name and residential address of each partner (unless professional association membership is verified)

controls.remediation: Your Collection of KYC Information procedure does not collect the minimum required information for partnerships. Ensure your procedure collects the partnership's full name, registered business name, country of establishment, individual partner information (per the individual ID procedure), and the name and residential address of each partner (unless regulated status is confirmed via professional association membership directory), as required under rule 4.5.3.

controls.label: Does your procedure include risk-based systems and controls to determine whether additional KYC information should be collected for partnerships?
controls.detail-label: Describe your risk-based assessment process for additional information collection
controls.remediation: Your Customer Identification Procedure for Partnerships does not include risk-based systems and controls to assess whether additional KYC information is needed. Implement a documented process to determine, based on assessed ML/TF risk, whether information beyond the minimum required under rule 4.5.3 should be collected, as required under rule 4.5.4.

controls.label: Does your procedure verify the partnership name from acceptable sources?

items.label: Certified copy or certified extract of partnership agreement
Reliable and independent documents relating to the partnership

controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity procedure does not verify the partnership name using acceptable sources. Ensure your procedure verifies the partnership's full name using at least one of: partnership agreement, certified copy/extract of partnership agreement, reliable and independent documents relating to the partnership, or reliable and independent electronic data, as required under rule 4.5.5(1).

controls.label: Does your procedure verify information about one of the partners in accordance with the applicable individual customer identification procedure?
controls.detail-label: Describe how you verify partner information
controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity procedure does not verify partner information according to the individual customer identification procedure. Ensure your procedure verifies information about at least one partner using the same identity verification methods required for individual customers under your AML/CTF program, as required under rule 4.5.5(2).

controls.label: Does your procedure include risk-based systems and controls to determine whether additional KYC information collected should be verified?
controls.detail-label: Describe your risk-based assessment process for additional information verification
controls.remediation: Your Customer Identification Procedure for Partnerships does not include risk-based systems and controls to assess whether additional collected KYC information should be verified. Implement a documented process to determine, based on assessed ML/TF risk, whether and to what extent information beyond the minimum required under rule 4.5.5 should be verified, as required under rule 4.5.6.

controls.label: Does your procedure limit verification of partnership information to the prescribed acceptable sources?

items.label: Certified copy or certified extract of partnership agreement
Certified copy or certified extract of minutes of a partnership meeting
Reliable and independent documents relating to the partnership

controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity procedure does not limit verification of partnership information to acceptable sources. Ensure that verification is based only on partnership agreements, certified copies/extracts of partnership agreements, certified copies/extracts of partnership meeting minutes, reliable and independent documents relating to the partnership, reliable and independent electronic data, or combinations thereof, as required under rule 4.5.7.

controls.label: Does your procedure recognize disclosure certificates as acceptable verification documents under specified conditions?

checklist-items.label: Verification is for purposes of additional KYC assessment (rule 4.5.6)
Information to be verified is not otherwise reasonably available from rule 4.5.7 sources

controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity procedure does not recognize disclosure certificates as acceptable verification documents. Update your procedure to accept disclosure certificates as reliable and independent documents for additional KYC information verification when: (1) the verification is for determining whether to verify additional collected information under rule 4.5.6, and (2) the information is not otherwise reasonably available from sources described in rule 4.5.7, as required under rule 4.5.8.

controls.label: Does your procedure include risk-based systems and controls to respond to discrepancies discovered during verification?
controls.detail-label: Describe your process for handling verification discrepancies
controls.remediation: Your Customer Identification Procedure for Partnerships does not include risk-based systems and controls to respond to verification discrepancies. Implement a documented process to handle discrepancies that arise during verification so that you can determine whether you are reasonably satisfied that the partnership exists and that all partner names have been provided (as required under rules 4.5.2(1) and 4.5.2(2)), as mandated by rule 4.5.9.

groups.title: Process Confirmation
groups.description: Before answering the detailed compliance questions, confirm whether your organisation operates this business process.

groups.title: General CDD Obligation
groups.description: Risk-based systems and controls to verify partnership existence and partner names.

groups.title: Collection of KYC Information
groups.description: Minimum KYC information and documentation to be collected about partnerships.

groups.title: Additional KYC Assessment
groups.description: Risk-based assessment of whether additional KYC information should be collected and verified.

groups.title: Verification of Identity
groups.description: Verification of partnership name and partner information.

groups.title: Verification Methods
groups.description: Prescribed acceptable sources for verifying partnership information.

groups.title: Disclosure Certificates
groups.description: Use of disclosure certificates for verifying partnership information under specified conditions.

groups.title: Responding to Discrepancies
groups.description: Risk-based systems and controls for handling discrepancies discovered during verification.
